title: Exmoor Verses II. Saturn
content:
From my farm, from h�r farm
        Furtively we came.
    In either home a hearth was warm:
        We nursed a hungrier flame.

    Our feet were foul with mire,
        Our faces blind with mist;
    But all the night was naked fire
        About us where we kiss'd.

    To her farm, to my farm,
        Loathing we returned;
    Pale beneath a gallow's arm
        The planet Saturn burned.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Arthur Thomas Quiller-Couch, titled "Exmoor Verses II. Saturn", represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
